data.name: Viking Therapeutics
data.brief: Clinical-stage biopharma with dual GLP-1/GIP agonist VK2735 (SC + oral) in obesity and VK0214 in X-ALD.

data.name: Novo Nordisk
data.brief: Co-leader of the GLP-1 market with semaglutide (Wegovy/Ozempic) and Phase 3 CagriSema.

data.name: Eli Lilly
data.brief: Co-leader with tirzepatide (Zepbound/Mounjaro). Broadest obesity pipeline: retatrutide (triple), orforglipron (oral).

data.name: Pfizer
data.brief: Discontinued oral GLP-1 danuglipron April 2025 after liver injury case. Re-entered obesity via $4.9B Metsera acquisition Nov 2025.

data.name: Amgen
data.brief: Developing MariTide (GLP-1 agonist + GIPR antagonist), an unusual mechanism. Phase 3 MARITIME initiated.

data.name: Roche
data.brief: Entered obesity via $3.1B Carmot acquisition Dec 2023. Assets: CT-388 (SC dual) and CT-996 (oral small molecule).

data.name: Boehringer Ingelheim
data.brief: Partnering with Zealand Pharma on survodutide (GLP-1 / glucagon dual agonist), Phase 3 obesity and MASH.

data.name: Zealand Pharma
data.brief: Partner on survodutide with BI. Pipeline includes amylin analog petrelintide and dapiglutide.

data.name: Structure Therapeutics
data.brief: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 agonist GSBR-209 (formerly aleniglipron). Phase 2a disappointment Jan 2026.

data.name: Express Scripts (Cigna)
data.brief: Pharmacy benefit manager. Tightened GLP-1 coverage with prior authorization requirements mid-2024.

data.name: GLP-1 receptor agonist
data.brief: Monotype GLP-1 receptor agonism. Class validated via semaglutide (Wegovy) and older liraglutide.

data.name: GIP / GLP-1 dual agonist
data.brief: Dual agonism of GIP and GLP-1 receptors. Validated by tirzepatide (Zepbound). Pursued by Viking VK2735.

data.name: GLP-1 + amylin
data.brief: Combination approach pairing GLP-1 with amylin analog (e.g., cagrilintide). Phase 3 for CagriSema.

data.name: GLP-1 / GIP / glucagon triple agonist
data.brief: Triple agonism adding glucagon receptor activity. Lilly retatrutide showed 24.2% weight loss Phase 2.

data.name: GLP-1 / glucagon dual
data.brief: Dual GLP-1 / glucagon agonism. Pursued by survodutide (BI/Zealand). Positioned for MASH as well as obesity.

data.name: GLP-1R agonist + GIPR antagonist
data.brief: Unusual mechanism pursued by Amgen MariTide. GIPR antagonism instead of agonism — hypothesis contested by tirzepatide success. Bone density concerns raised.

data.name: VK2735 (subcutaneous)
data.moa_summary: Dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist, weekly SC dosing.
data.brief: Viking's lead obesity asset. VENTURE Phase 2 showed 14.7% weight loss at 13 weeks. VANQUISH Phase 3 enrolling.
data.description: Viking Therapeutics' dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist in Phase 3 for obesity. VENTURE Phase 2 data (Feb 2024) reported 14.7% mean weight loss at 13 weeks vs placebo at the highest dose, with favorable tolerability. VANQUISH Phase 3 (NCT06781983) initiated in 2025.

data.name: VK2735 (oral)
data.moa_summary: Oral tablet formulation of dual GLP-1/GIP agonist.
data.brief: Oral formulation of VK2735. Phase 1 showed dose-dependent weight loss; Phase 2 ongoing with readout expected H2 2026.

data.name: semaglutide (Wegovy / Ozempic)
data.moa_summary: GLP-1 receptor agonist, weekly SC dosing.
data.brief: Novo's market-leading GLP-1 for obesity (Wegovy) and T2D (Ozempic). SELECT trial established CV benefit.

data.name: tirzepatide (Zepbound / Mounjaro)
data.moa_summary: Dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist, weekly SC dosing.
data.brief: Lilly's dual GLP-1/GIP agonist. SURMOUNT-5 head-to-head demonstrated 47% greater weight loss vs semaglutide.

data.name: CagriSema
data.moa_summary: Fixed-dose combination of amylin analog cagrilintide and GLP-1 agonist semaglutide.
data.brief: Novo's flagship next-gen candidate. REDEFINE 1 Phase 3 reported 22.7% weight loss — below consensus, triggering $125B market cap loss on readout.

data.name: orforglipron
data.moa_summary: Non-peptide small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist, once-daily oral dosing.
data.brief: Lilly's oral small-molecule GLP-1. ATTAIN-1 Phase 3 reported 14.7% weight loss at 72 weeks (Aug 2025) — first oral to match injectable-class efficacy.

data.name: retatrutide
data.moa_summary: Triple GLP-1 / GIP / glucagon receptor agonist.
data.brief: Lilly's triple agonist. Phase 2 showed 24.2% weight loss at 48 weeks — sets the efficacy ceiling for the class.

data.name: MariTide
data.moa_summary: GLP-1 receptor agonist + GIPR antagonist bispecific, monthly SC dosing.
data.brief: Amgen's bispecific with GIPR antagonism (not agonism). Phase 2 ~20% weight loss at 52 weeks. Monthly dosing as differentiator.

data.name: danuglipron
data.brief: Pfizer's oral GLP-1. Discontinued April 2025 after drug-induced liver injury case in Phase 3 participant.
data.description: Pfizer discontinued development of danuglipron in April 2025 following a case of drug-induced liver injury in a Phase 3 participant, despite favorable efficacy data. The termination followed earlier 2023 discontinuation of the twice-daily formulation for tolerability issues.

data.name: survodutide
data.moa_summary: Dual GLP-1 / glucagon receptor agonist, weekly SC dosing.
data.brief: BI / Zealand Pharma dual agonist. Phase 2 obesity 18.7% weight loss at 46 weeks. Also in MASH Phase 3.

data.name: GSBR-209 (aleniglipron)
data.brief: Structure Therapeutics' oral GLP-1. Phase 2a reported Jan 2026 came in below expectations, pressuring oral small-molecule thesis.

data.name: CT-996
data.moa_summary: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist, acquired from Carmot.
data.brief: Roche's oral GLP-1 from the Carmot acquisition. Phase 1 data showed 7.3% weight loss at 4 weeks.

data.name: VENTURE (VK2735 SC Phase 2)
data.primary_endpoint: Percent change in body weight at 13 weeks
data.brief: Viking VK2735 SC Phase 2 in obesity. 14.7% weight loss at highest dose vs 1.7% placebo.

data.name: VANQUISH (VK2735 SC Phase 3)
data.primary_endpoint: Percent change in body weight at 72 weeks
data.brief: Viking VK2735 SC Phase 3. Interim analysis planned for H2 2026.

data.name: VK2735 Oral Phase 2
data.brief: Viking VK2735 oral tablet Phase 2. Readout expected H2 2026.

data.name: REDEFINE 1 (CagriSema)
data.primary_endpoint: Percent change in body weight at 68 weeks
data.brief: Novo CagriSema Phase 3. 22.7% weight loss — below consensus of ~25%. Triggered major selloff.

data.name: ATTAIN-1 (orforglipron)
data.primary_endpoint: Percent change in body weight at 72 weeks
data.brief: Lilly orforglipron Phase 3. 14.7% weight loss — first oral to match injectable class.

data.name: TRIUMPH-4 (retatrutide)
data.brief: Lilly retatrutide Phase 3 obesity program.

data.name: SURMOUNT-5 (tirzepatide vs semaglutide)
data.design: 72-week randomized open-label head-to-head tirzepatide vs semaglutide in obesity
data.primary_endpoint: Percent change in body weight at 72 weeks
data.brief: First head-to-head in the class. Tirzepatide 20.2% vs semaglutide 13.7% weight loss (47% greater).

data.name: MARITIME (MariTide Phase 3)
data.brief: Amgen MariTide Phase 3 obesity program. Tests GLP-1R agonist + GIPR antagonist bispecific hypothesis.

data.name: Roche acquires Carmot Therapeutics
data.brief: Roche entered obesity with $2.7B upfront, up to $400M milestones for Carmot's CT-388 (SC dual) and CT-996 (oral).

data.name: Lilly acquires Versanis Bio
data.brief: Lilly acquired Versanis for bimagrumab (activin receptor antagonist, muscle-sparing weight-loss adjunct) for up to $1.925B.

data.name: Pfizer acquires Metsera
data.brief: After danuglipron discontinuation, Pfizer paid up to $4.9B for Metsera's amylin-agonist and ultra-long-acting GLP-1 portfolio to re-enter obesity.

data.name: FDA expands Wegovy label to CV risk reduction
data.brief: FDA approved Wegovy label update to reduce risk of MACE in overweight/obese adults with established CVD, based on SELECT trial.
data.description: The FDA on March 8, 2024 approved a new indication for Wegovy (semaglutide 2.4 mg) to reduce the risk of cardiovascular death, heart attack, and stroke in adults with cardiovascular disease and obesity or overweight. The approval was based on the SELECT trial.

data.name: FDA approves Zepbound for obstructive sleep apnea
data.brief: First drug approved for moderate-to-severe OSA in obese adults. Expands addressable population and strengthens payer case.

data.name: CMS memo: Medicare Part D can cover GLP-1 for CVD indication
data.brief: CMS clarified that anti-obesity medications with an approved CV indication (e.g., Wegovy) may be covered under Medicare Part D.

data.name: FDA class label update for GLP-1 GI adverse events
data.brief: FDA added ileus to GLP-1 class labeling after post-marketing reports. Baseline GI safety signal for the class.

data.name: GLP-1 Pipeline Explosion
data.brief: 15+ companies racing in obesity. M&A, platform deals, mechanism diversification — conviction money signals the addressable market is the largest in pharma.
data.description: The obesity market validated by semaglutide and tirzepatide has become the most crowded race in pharma. Novo, Lilly, Amgen, Roche, Pfizer, Boehringer, and 20+ biotechs including Viking, Structure, and Zealand are pursuing the space across mechanisms (pure GLP-1, GIP dual, triple, glucagon, amylin combos) and routes (SC, oral small molecule, oral peptide). Deal activity signals conviction: Roche/Carmot $3.1B, Pfizer/Metsera $4.9B, Lilly/Versanis $1.9B.

data.name: Oral GLP-1 Displaces Injectable
data.brief: Thesis: oral formulations will unlock primary-care adoption, expanding addressable population beyond specialty channels. Mixed evidence through 2026.
data.description: The strategic bet that oral GLP-1 formulations will displace injectable in primary care by reducing administration friction. Supported by Lilly orforglipron Phase 3 (ATTAIN-1) matching injectable class efficacy. Challenged by Pfizer danuglipron discontinuation (hepatotoxicity) and Structure GSBR-209 Phase 2a disappointment. Viking's oral VK2735 is a key open bet.

data.name: Efficacy Ceiling Debate
data.brief: Is there a weight-loss ceiling? CagriSema 22.7% disappointed at the consensus 25%. Retatrutide hit 24.2%. Market is re-pricing incremental efficacy vs tolerability.
data.description: After tirzepatide's 20% bar, the market priced Phase 3 programs expecting 25%+ weight loss. CagriSema's 22.7% readout — a clinically meaningful result historically, but below consensus — crashed Novo stock and reset expectations. Simultaneously, retatrutide Phase 2 reached 24.2%, arguing the ceiling is higher but requires mechanism expansion. The emerging question: is incremental efficacy worth tolerability trade-offs?

data.name: Payer Pushback on GLP-1 Pricing
data.brief: Payers tightening access at current prices (~$1,000/month pre-rebate). Express Scripts prior-auth. CMS clarifications. Long-term affordability unresolved.
data.description: At current list prices, GLP-1 coverage is economically challenging when 40%+ of US adults qualify. Express Scripts moved GLP-1s to prior authorization mid-2024. CMS clarified Part D coverage is possible for CV-indicated use, avoiding a broader Medicare expansion. Label expansions (CV, OSA) support payer case. The structural question — can GLP-1 pricing hold at population scale — remains open.

data.name: Pfizer discontinues danuglipron after liver injury case
data.brief: April 2025. Pfizer ends oral GLP-1 program after drug-induced liver injury in Phase 3 participant. Resolved case but terminated development.
data.description: Pfizer announced April 14, 2025 that it would discontinue development of danuglipron, its modified-release oral GLP-1 agonist, following a case of potential drug-induced liver injury in a Phase 3 participant. The injury resolved after drug discontinuation, but Pfizer chose to terminate the program. This was the second setback for danuglipron — the twice-daily formulation had been discontinued in 2023 for tolerability.

data.name: CagriSema REDEFINE 1: 22.7% weight loss misses consensus
data.brief: Dec 2024. Novo's flagship next-gen asset reports 22.7% weight loss — clinically excellent, below ~25% market expectation. Novo stock -18% on the day.
data.description: On December 20, 2024, Novo Nordisk reported REDEFINE 1 Phase 3 results for CagriSema (cagrilintide + semaglutide) in obesity: 22.7% mean weight loss at 68 weeks versus ~3% placebo. While clinically superior to semaglutide monotherapy (Wegovy ~15%), the result fell below sell-side consensus expectations of ~25%. Novo shares fell ~18% intraday. The event reset class expectations and raised questions about whether efficacy is hitting diminishing returns.

data.name: Viking VENTURE Phase 2: 14.7% weight loss at 13 weeks
data.brief: Feb 2024. VK2735 SC 13-week dose-ranging showed 14.7% weight loss at highest dose vs 1.7% placebo. Projected trajectory to ~20%+ at injectable competitor timepoints.
data.description: Viking Therapeutics reported VENTURE Phase 2 top-line data on February 27, 2024: VK2735 SC produced 14.7% mean weight loss at 13 weeks at the highest dose (15 mg) versus 1.7% for placebo. Tolerability profile was favorable. At the same 13-week timepoint, semaglutide had produced ~6% and tirzepatide ~8% in their respective programs, implying VK2735 could exceed 20% weight loss at the 68-72 week endpoint used by approved products.

data.name: Viking initiates VANQUISH Phase 3 for VK2735 SC
data.brief: March 2025. VANQUISH Phase 3 registration study initiated. 4,500 patients, 72-week endpoint, interim planned H2 2026.
data.description: Viking announced the initiation of VANQUISH, its pivotal Phase 3 trial of VK2735 subcutaneous in obesity (NCT06781983). The trial enrolls approximately 4,500 participants across multiple dose arms against placebo, with primary endpoint at 72 weeks. An interim analysis is planned for H2 2026 to assess efficacy and futility.

data.name: Viking initiates VK2735 oral Phase 2
data.brief: Nov 2024. Oral VK2735 tablet Phase 2 registered. Readout expected H2 2026.
data.description: Viking initiated Phase 2 of its oral VK2735 tablet formulation in obesity (NCT06153758), following Phase 1 data demonstrating dose-dependent weight loss over 28 days.

data.name: Lilly orforglipron ATTAIN-1 Phase 3: 14.7% weight loss
data.brief: Aug 2025. First oral GLP-1 to match injectable-class efficacy. 14.7% at 72 weeks. Validates oral thesis; sets competitive bar for Viking oral.
data.description: Lilly reported ATTAIN-1 Phase 3 top-line results in August 2025: orforglipron, an oral non-peptide small-molecule GLP-1 agonist, produced 14.7% mean weight loss at 72 weeks versus placebo in adults with obesity. The result establishes the first oral GLP-1 to match injectable-class efficacy and positions Lilly for an oral commercial launch ahead of peer oral programs.

data.name: Lilly retatrutide Phase 2: 24.2% weight loss
data.brief: Nov 2023. Triple agonist Phase 2 sets new class ceiling at 24.2% at 48 weeks. Establishes that mechanism expansion can go higher.
data.description: Lilly reported Phase 2 data for retatrutide (GLP-1/GIP/glucagon triple agonist) showing 24.2% mean weight loss at 48 weeks at the highest dose — the largest weight loss ever reported for a pharmaceutical intervention in obesity. The result anchored the class ceiling above CagriSema's later 22.7% Phase 3 readout.

data.name: SURMOUNT-5: tirzepatide beats semaglutide head-to-head
data.brief: Dec 2024. Tirzepatide 20.2% vs semaglutide 13.7% — 47% greater weight loss. First head-to-head; validates GIP/GLP-1 dual advantage.
data.description: SURMOUNT-5 (NCT05822830), the first head-to-head Phase 3 between tirzepatide and semaglutide in obesity, reported December 2024 results showing 20.2% weight loss at 72 weeks for tirzepatide vs 13.7% for semaglutide — a 47% relative advantage. The result validated GIP/GLP-1 dual agonism as superior to GLP-1 monotherapy and anchored Viking's own dual-agonist thesis for VK2735.

data.name: Amgen MariTide Phase 2: ~20% weight loss, durability debated
data.brief: March 2025. MariTide 52-week Phase 2 reported 20% weight loss; monthly dosing as differentiator; weight regain after discontinuation raised questions.
data.description: Amgen reported MariTide Phase 2 results in March 2025: ~20% mean weight loss at 52 weeks with its GLP-1R agonist + GIPR antagonist bispecific at monthly dosing. The monthly administration is a commercial differentiator, but post-hoc data showing weight regain after discontinuation and a weaker tail effect raised questions about durability. Phase 3 MARITIME initiated subsequently.

data.name: Roche acquires Carmot Therapeutics for $3.1B
data.brief: Dec 2023. Roche enters obesity via Carmot acquisition. CT-388 (SC dual) and CT-996 (oral) bring Roche to three obesity candidates.
data.description: Roche announced acquisition of Carmot Therapeutics on December 4, 2023 for $2.7B upfront and up to $400M in contingent milestones. The deal brought CT-388 (SC dual GLP-1/GIP) and CT-996 (oral small molecule GLP-1) into Roche's pipeline, establishing Roche as a late entrant with multiple shots on goal.

data.name: Pfizer acquires Metsera for $4.9B
data.brief: Nov 2025. After danuglipron, Pfizer re-enters obesity via Metsera. Amylin-agonist and ultra-long-acting GLP-1 portfolio.
data.description: Pfizer announced acquisition of Metsera in November 2025 for up to $4.9B, approximately seven months after discontinuing danuglipron. Metsera's assets include MET-097i (ultra-long-acting GLP-1 agonist, monthly dosing) and MET-233i (amylin analog), positioning Pfizer back in obesity with differentiated mechanisms.

data.name: FDA expands Wegovy label to CV risk reduction
data.brief: March 2024. SELECT-based label expansion. Unlocks Medicare Part D coverage for CV-indicated use. Strengthens structural payer case for the class.
data.description: FDA approved a label expansion for Wegovy on March 8, 2024 to include reduction of cardiovascular events (MACE) in overweight/obese adults with established CVD, based on SELECT trial results (20% MACE reduction). The new indication unlocked Medicare Part D coverage for this population via subsequent CMS guidance.

data.name: Express Scripts adds prior authorization for GLP-1 obesity use
data.brief: June 2024. Largest PBM moves GLP-1 anti-obesity indication to prior-auth tier. Signals broader payer resistance at current pricing.
data.description: Express Scripts implemented prior-authorization requirements for GLP-1 medications prescribed for obesity (without diabetes or CVD) in mid-2024, citing budget impact and the need for step therapy documentation. The move by the largest US PBM signaled broader payer resistance to unrestricted coverage of anti-obesity GLP-1s at list prices.

data.name: CMS allows Medicare Part D coverage for GLP-1 in CVD indication
data.brief: March 2024. CMS memo clarified Part D plans may cover anti-obesity GLP-1s with approved CV indication. Partial unlock; excludes obesity alone.
data.description: Following Wegovy's CV label expansion, CMS issued guidance on March 21, 2024 clarifying that Medicare Part D plans may cover anti-obesity medications with an FDA-approved secondary indication (e.g., CV risk reduction). The guidance preserved the statutory exclusion for weight loss alone but created a coverage pathway for the CV-indicated population.

data.name: FDA approves Zepbound for obstructive sleep apnea
data.brief: Dec 2024. First OSA drug approval. Expands addressable population and payer coverage case for tirzepatide specifically.
data.description: FDA approved Zepbound (tirzepatide) on December 20, 2024 as the first drug for moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea in obese adults, based on SURMOUNT-OSA trial. The approval adds a medically-indicated population that is more favorable for payer coverage than weight loss alone.

data.name: Viking Q4 2025 earnings: cash runway through VANQUISH readout
data.brief: Feb 2026. Viking reported ~$800M cash, runway extending beyond VANQUISH interim. Removed near-term financing overhang.
data.description: Viking Therapeutics Q4 2025 earnings reported approximately $800M in cash and equivalents as of December 31, 2025, with operating cash runway sufficient to reach VANQUISH interim readout and VK2735 oral Phase 2 readout, removing a near-term equity dilution concern.

data.name: Structure GSBR-209 Phase 2a disappoints
data.brief: Jan 2026. Structure's oral small-molecule GLP-1 underperformed expectations in Phase 2a. Pressures oral small-molecule category beyond Lilly's orforglipron.
data.description: Structure Therapeutics reported Phase 2a data for GSBR-209 (aleniglipron) in January 2026 that came in below sell-side expectations, with weight loss in the range of prior oral candidates but without the differentiated profile investors had priced in. Structure stock fell ~35% on the readout. The result raised questions about whether oral small molecules beyond Lilly's orforglipron can reach competitive efficacy.

data.name: Viking secures CMO capacity for VK2735 SC manufacturing
data.brief: Sept 2025. Viking signed two CMOs for drug substance and drug product scale-up to support VANQUISH Phase 3.
data.description: Viking Therapeutics announced in September 2025 that it had executed manufacturing agreements with two contract manufacturing organizations (CMOs) for drug substance and drug product scale-up of VK2735, supporting VANQUISH Phase 3 supply needs and potential commercial launch readiness.

data.name: Mechanism is validated; formulation is the moat
data.brief: Every major GLP-1 program is converging on similar efficacy (14-24% weight loss). Differentiation is shifting to formulation (oral, monthly, tolerability) rather than absolute weight loss.
data.importance: Strategic: reframes competitive differentiation away from efficacy arms race toward delivery, dosing frequency, and safety profile.
data.description: Across the class, mechanisms are increasingly validated: GLP-1 monotherapy (~15%), GIP/GLP-1 dual (~20%), triple agonist (~24%). The efficacy band is narrow. What differs dramatically is route, dosing frequency (weekly vs monthly), tolerability, and cost of goods. Viking's competitive position rests less on being the most efficacious and more on executing best-in-class tolerability and getting oral to market competitively.

data.name: Pfizer danuglipron failure is formulation, not mechanism
data.brief: Danuglipron discontinuation was driven by hepatotoxicity specific to its chemical structure, not general oral GLP-1 risk. Distinguishing formulation risk from mechanism risk is critical.
data.importance: Clinical / Strategic: determines whether Pfizer's failure has read-through to Viking's oral program (different molecular scaffold, different peptide modifications).

data.name: CagriSema 'miss' is about expectations, not an efficacy ceiling
data.brief: 22.7% weight loss is clinically excellent. The 'miss' was vs sell-side consensus of 25%. Retatrutide 24.2% argues the ceiling is higher.
data.importance: Market / Clinical: the efficacy ceiling debate is contingent on mechanism choice, not a biological hard limit. Suggests Viking shouldn't over-index on matching retatrutide.

data.name: FDA approves Wegovy for CV risk reduction
CMS Part D memo on GLP-1 coverage
VK2735 Subcutaneous Program
data.brief: Viking's lead obesity program. VK2735 SC weekly injection. VANQUISH Phase 3 enrolling, interim readout H2 2026.
data.description: The subcutaneous formulation of VK2735 is Viking Therapeutics' lead obesity asset. Following VENTURE Phase 2 readout in 2024 (14.7% at 13 weeks), Viking initiated VANQUISH Phase 3 in March 2025. Interim analysis planned for H2 2026. A go/no-go decision point gates the continuation of the full Phase 3 program.

data.name: VK2735 Oral Program
data.brief: Viking's oral tablet formulation of VK2735. Phase 2 ongoing, readout expected H2 2026. Would compete with Lilly orforglipron.
data.description: The oral VK2735 program is Viking's second bet: an oral peptide GLP-1/GIP dual agonist tablet. Phase 1 was positive; Phase 2 began November 2024. Readout expected H2 2026. Positioning is against Lilly's orforglipron (Phase 3 positive Aug 2025) for the primary-care oral GLP-1 market.

data.name: VK2735 has best-in-class GI tolerability
data.statement: VK2735 SC will demonstrate superior GI tolerability (fewer grade 3+ AEs, lower discontinuation rate) vs tirzepatide and semaglutide, enabling faster titration and higher mean exposure in real-world use.

data.name: VK2735 SC efficacy matches or beats tirzepatide
data.statement: VK2735 SC will produce ≥20% weight loss at 68+ weeks, matching or exceeding tirzepatide (SURMOUNT-1: 20.9% at 72 weeks).

data.name: Oral GLP-1 will displace injectable in primary care
data.statement: Oral GLP-1 formulations will unlock primary-care prescribing and expand the addressable population by 3-5x vs injectable-only, within 5 years of first oral approval.

data.name: Dual GLP-1/GIP mechanism is safe at scale
data.statement: The dual GLP-1/GIP agonism mechanism, already validated by tirzepatide at commercial scale, is safe across hepatic, cardiovascular, and GI dimensions and is not subject to the hepatotoxicity seen with danuglipron.

data.name: Payer coverage will expand, not contract, at current pricing
data.statement: Payer coverage of GLP-1 anti-obesity use will expand over the next 3 years at approximately current list pricing, driven by label expansions (CV, OSA) and population health ROI evidence.

data.name: VANQUISH interim readout go/no-go
data.brief: Go/no-go on continuing VANQUISH Phase 3 at interim, based on efficacy and futility analysis.
data.description: At the VANQUISH Phase 3 interim analysis (planned H2 2026), Viking and its board will decide whether to continue the full Phase 3 program based on interim efficacy, safety, and futility data. A go decision accelerates toward BLA submission. A pause or adjustment could imply a dose optimization or design change.

data.name: Move VK2735 oral to Phase 3
data.brief: Decision to initiate Phase 3 of VK2735 oral, contingent on Phase 2 readout and competitive positioning vs orforglipron.

data.name: Does Pfizer danuglipron's hepatotoxicity translate to Viking oral?
data.brief: Differentiating whether Pfizer's liver injury was mechanism-level (read-through to oral GLP-1 class) or formulation-level (specific to danuglipron's chemistry).
data.description: Pfizer danuglipron's Phase 3 discontinuation after drug-induced liver injury raises a binary question for Viking's oral program: is the hepatotoxicity inherent to oral GLP-1 exposure (mechanism-level, implying read-through to VK2735 oral), or specific to danuglipron's chemical scaffold (formulation-level, implying Viking is safe). The evidence distinguishes the two: danuglipron is a small-molecule with a distinct hepatic metabolism profile; VK2735 is a peptide with different disposition.

data.name: Does Lilly orforglipron close Viking's oral competitive window?
data.brief: With orforglipron Phase 3 positive Aug 2025 and launch targeted for 2026-27, what competitive window remains for VK2735 oral?
data.description: Lilly's orforglipron ATTAIN-1 Phase 3 positive readout (14.7% weight loss, Aug 2025) positions it for regulatory submission and commercial launch potentially 2026-27. VK2735 oral Phase 2 readout is expected H2 2026, with Phase 3 initiation possible late 2026. Question: is a 2-3 year launch gap survivable with differentiation, or is the oral primary-care market captured by Lilly before Viking arrives?

data.name: Is payer coverage of GLP-1 expanding or contracting at current pricing?
data.brief: Mixed signals: CV label expansion + CMS memo expand coverage; Express Scripts prior-auth tightens it. Net direction matters for Viking's commercial model.

data.name: Can CMO capacity meet Phase 3 VANQUISH demand?
data.brief: Two CMO agreements signed September 2025 resolved near-term capacity concerns for VANQUISH scale-up.
